title: Sub Organisations Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the sub organisations capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Sub-organisation lifecycle is list active child organisations, create with a slug and display name, update the display name, and deactivate by clearing the active flag. Deactivation is a soft state change rather than a hard delete.

stateModel.description: State is an organisation row carrying parent id, slug, display name, active flag, and timestamps, exposed to the API as a flat tenant-scoped list while the parent id is kept internal. Children are always anchored to a resolved parent tenant.

permissions.description: Routes require the specific sub-organisation read, create, update, or delete permission, are tenant-scoped, and declare a sub-organisation resource and matching scopes. Tenant isolation is enforced by filtering on the resolved parent id.

contracts.description: The operation surface lists and creates sub-organisations and updates or deactivates a sub-organisation by id. Mutations return typed results mapped to standard responses.

validation.description: Creation validates a lowercase slug's length, characters, and no edge hyphen, validates display-name length, rejects reserved slugs, and rejects a duplicate global slug, while update requires at least a display name with a length check. Invalid input is rejected before any change.

errorModel.description: Typed outcomes include invalid-body, reserved-slug, conflict, not-found, and ok, mapped fail-closed to 400/422/409/404/201/200/204; provider failure returns a typed degraded result and keeps mutation boundaries closed. No partial child state is persisted on failure.

auditModel.description: Create, update, and deactivate emit sub-organisation created, updated, and deactivated audit-before-change events, and audit failure aborts the insert, update, or deactivation. Read-only listing is not audited.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is invariant-ready once the organisations table is available; because that table has no row-level security, every query explicitly filters by the parent id from the resolved tenant context, and degraded mode disables store-dependent mutation while keeping read-only listing visible.

proof.description: A unit proof covers the state machine and validation and a UI semantic harness proves the headless journey, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic state/validation proof plus a semantic harness for future UI generation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; the source-lineage headless sub-organisation journey is lineage only. Author the UI semantic model before generation.
